content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Comrnission
March 14, 199O
Leo B. Ner.rton
Attorney at Law
B13O East Florence Avenue
Downey, CA 9O24O
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-113
Dear Mr. Newton 3
This is in response to your request for advice concerning the
contribution limitations of the Political- Reform Act (the rrActrr). r'
OUESTIONS
1. You are the treasurer and sole nember of a non-controlled
comnittee that contributes to judicial candidates. Can the
committee make contributions to a judicial'candidate who has asked
you to be the treasurer of his carnpaign conrnittee?
2. If the answer to Question f- is yes, is there a limit 
on
the anount your cornrnittee can contribute to the judicial
cand idate?
3. If your comrnittee makes a contribution to the judicial
candidate, does it make a difference as to \,rhether you can serve
as treasurer for that candidate/s campaign conrnittee?
CONCLUS ION
1, The fact that a candidate has asked you to serve as
treasurer for his carnpaign committee does not preclude you from
naking a contribution to that candidate from your own non-
candidate controlled committee.
1 cor".nr"nt code sections B1oo0-9101-5. Arl, statutory references
are to the covernment code unfess otherwise indicated. 
Commissi-on
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et ses. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Requfations.
42SJStreet,Suite8OO . P.O.Box80? O SacramentoCA 958O4.O8O7 . (gt6)3ZZ-566O


Our File No. A-9O-113
Page 2
2. There is a timit on how much your committee may
contribute to a candidate during a fiscal year. From the facts
presented, it appears that your corumittee nay contribute a maximum
of $1,000 to a candidate during a fiscal year.
3. You may act as treasurer for the judicial candidate's
carnpaign committee whether or not your cornrnittee has contributed
to that candidate.
FACTS
You are treasurer and sole member of a committee identified
as "Conmittee for the Independence of Downey Court.rr This
committee is not a candidate-control fed committee but exists to,
arnong other things, make contributj-ons to candidates for judicial
office.
You have recently been asked by a judicial candidate to act
as the treasurer for his campaign committee.
ANALYS I S
Sections 85100 through 85307 set forth the only linitations
in the Act on rnaking contributions to candidates for elective
office. 
These limitations apply equally to candidates for
el-ection to judicial .rlu ,lon-judicial oifice.2
While these provisions place linitations 
on the amounts that
can be contributed (see Sections 85301- through 85303 and 85305)
and the use of contributions (see sections 85201 , 85202 and
85304),3 neither these nor any other provisions of the Act
prohibit a person from sirnultaneously running a politicat 
action
cornmittee and serving as treasurer for a candidate to whom the
committee makes or may rnake contributions. 
Therefore, the Act
does not prevent you fron :naking contributions to the judicial
candidate on behalf of your committee and sirnultaneously serving
as treasurer for that candidate's campaign committee.
2 sections 85100 through 85307 make repeated reference to
rrcandidatesrr for office. 
Section 82007 defines rtcandidate,'r in
pertinent part, as "an individual who is listed on the bal1qt ...
for riomination for or el-ection to anv elective office, or who
receives a contribution ... with a view to bringing about his or
her nornination or electj.on to anv elective office, whether or not
the specific elective office ... is known at the time the
contribution is received.
rr (Emphasis added. )
- Section 85306 v,,as invalidated in the case of Service Employees
International Union, et aI. v. Fair pol-itical practices
Commission,, U.S. District court, Eastern District of California,
case No. 89-0433 LKK-JFIU.


Our File No. A-9 0-113
Page 3
However, the Act does impose f irnits upon the amount your
cornrnittee may make in contributions to the judicial candidate.
Depending on the composition of a particular committee, it may
make a rnaximum contribution of $1,o0o, 92,5oo or $5,0o0 to a
candidate in a fiscal year.{' Since your committee is operated
solely by you and not in conjunction with one or more other
persoirs,-i€ does not appear fo qualify as a political 
cornmittee5
or broad based political 
cornmittee6 under the Act's contribution
limits. 
Accordingly, it is cLassified as a person, which is
defined in section 85102(b) as:
tAln individual, proprietorshj-p, f irrn, partnership,
joint venture, syndicate, business trust, 
company,
corporation, association, comm j-ttee, and labor
organization.
(Emphasis added . )
Section 85301(a) sets forth the lirnits on contributions frorn
persons to candidates, stating:
No person sha1l rnake, and no candidate for elective
office, or campaign treasurer, shafl solicit 
or
accept any contribution or loan which would cause
the total amount contributed or loaned by that
person to that candidate, including contributions
or loans to all comnittees controlle{ by the
candidate, to exceed one thousand dollars ($1,000)
in any fiscal year.
Therefore, if your cornmittee chooses to make a contribution to the
judicial candidate, or any candidate for elective office, it may
only give that candidate a maximun of $l-,000 in a fj-scal year.
FinalIy, please note that, for the purpose of calculating the
$1,000 contribution linit, 
any contribution made to a candidate by
your cornmittee will be cumulated with any contributions you make
to the same candidate from your ohrn personal funds. In its
i' Section 85102 (a) defines trfiscal yearrr as the period from
July 1 through June 30.
q 
.'
" Section 85L02 (c) defines ttpoJ,itical conmitteerr as Ia connittee
of persons who receive contributions fron two or more persons and
actinq in concert rnakes contributions to candidates.r 
(Emphasis
added. )
" Section 85102 (d) defines rrbroad based political 
committeer as
rra comrnittee of persons which has been in existence for more than
six months, receives contributions from one hundred or nore
persons, and actinq .in concert nakes contributions to five or more
candidates.'r (Emphasis added, )


Our File No. A-9 0- 113
Page 4
opinion entitled In re Lumsdon (L976) 2 FPPC ops. 140 (copy
enclosed), the commission concluded that cumufation was required
where separate contributions were made by a corporation and the
corporation's rnajority shareholder. The rationale for this
conclusion $ras that one person was in fact controllinq both his
personal contributions and those of the corporation,
Subsequent decisions by the Comrnission and its staff have
required cumulation of contributions vrhere the factual
circunstances have slightly differed but the general rationale was
still 
rel-evant. (see rn re Kahn (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 150
(contributions by parent company and wholly owned subsi.diary);
Regulation 18531.5 (contributions by two or more entities where
the same person in fact directs and controls the entities'
contribution decisions) ; Leidiqh Advice Letter, No. I-89-637
(contributions by a law firrn and its partners) , copies enclosed. )
The underlying rationale for these decisions is present in your
situation. 
You alone control both your personal contributions and
those of your cornmittee. Accordingly, any contributions nade by
your committee must be cunulated with any personal contributions
you may make to the same candidate.
should you have additional questions, please contact rne at
(916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
Kathry'r1 E. Donovan
counsel, Legal Division
KED:SH:ld
